latestPlannerResponse.message: Here is the plan for Scaffold routes and independent placeholder pages for PoC screens.
latestPlannerResponse.featureUnderstanding: Scaffold seven public routes in the Rivvy Portal UI (React/TS with React Router) that each render a distinct, minimal placeholder page. Routes: /login, /profile, /dashboard, /new-brief, /vault, /screening, /admin. Each page shows a visible title matching its screen name and includes a unique data-testid for automated verification. A shared minimal layout may be used. No authentication, role-aware routing, global navigation, or business logic is included.

in: Add React Router routes in Rivvy Portal UI for /login, /profile, /dashboard, /new-brief, /vault, /screening, /admin
Create distinct placeholder pages for each route rendered within a shared minimal layout
Display a visible title on each page matching: Login, Profile, Dashboard, New Brief, Brand Vault, Screening Room, Admin
Include a unique data-testid on each placeholder page
Ensure direct navigation to each path loads the corresponding page without redirects or errors
out: API calls, state management, or integration with Rivvy Portal Services
Redirect logic, 404/500 handling changes, or error boundary work
Conventions or placement rules for data-testid attributes
Updates to architecture model entities or UI workflow diagrams

assumptions: The development server/build setup supports client-side routing for deep links to all listed paths.
The implementer will choose the element and naming for each data-testid while ensuring uniqueness.
Titles should match exactly: Login, Profile, Dashboard, New Brief, Brand Vault, Screening Room, Admin.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /login renders a placeholder with visible title 'Login' and a unique data-testid; no redirects or guards occur.
Navigating to /profile renders a placeholder with visible title 'Profile' and a unique data-testid; no redirects or guards occur.
Navigating to /dashboard renders a placeholder with visible title 'Dashboard' and a unique data-testid; no redirects or guards occur.
Navigating to /new-brief renders a placeholder with visible title 'New Brief' and a unique data-testid; no redirects or guards occur.
Navigating to /vault renders a placeholder with visible title 'Brand Vault' and a unique data-testid; no redirects or guards occur.
Navigating to /screening renders a placeholder with visible title 'Screening Room' and a unique data-testid; no redirects or guards occur.
Navigating to /admin renders a placeholder with visible title 'Admin' and a unique data-testid; no redirects or guards occur.
Each placeholder page uses a distinct data-testid value not reused by other pages.
No global navigation, role-aware routing, or business logic is introduced by this work.
Placeholders may render within a shared layout but must remain visually distinguishable by their titles.

implementationPlan.planTitle: Implementation Plan for Scaffold routes and independent placeholder pages for PoC screens

increments.title: Add React Router routes and placeholder pages with shared minimal layout
increments.intent:
- Tech stack: React (TypeScript) with React Router v6+.
- Goal: Define seven public routes and minimal placeholder pages with exact titles and unique data-testids; no guards, no navigation, no business logic.

Implementation steps:
1) Dependencies and setup
  - Ensure react-router-dom is installed (v6+). If missing: npm i react-router-dom or yarn add react-router-dom.
  - Confirm the app root (e.g., src/main.tsx or src/index.tsx) renders <App /> inside React.StrictMode and supports client-side routing.

2) Shared minimal layout
  - Create a minimal shared layout component (e.g., src/layouts/MinimalLayout.tsx) with:
    - A simple container element (e.g., <div> or <main>) and React Router <Outlet /> to render child routes.
    - No global nav, header, or footer beyond what’s necessary to display page content.

3) Placeholder pages (distinct components)
  - Create one component per route (location flexible, e.g., src/pages or src/screens). Each component should:
    - Render a visible title matching exactly the screen name.
    - Include a unique data-testid on a deterministic parent or title element (implementer’s choice) and ensure uniqueness across pages.
  - Pages and their titles (examples in parentheses for suggested data-testid values; implementer may choose different names):
    - Login — title: "Login" (e.g., data-testid: page-login)
    - Profile — title: "Profile" (e.g., data-testid: page-profile)
    - Dashboard — title: "Dashboard" (e.g., data-testid: page-dashboard)
    - New Brief — title: "New Brief" (e.g., data-testid: page-new-brief)
    - Brand Vault — title: "Brand Vault" (e.g., data-testid: page-vault)
    - Screening Room — title: "Screening Room" (e.g., data-testid: page-screening)
    - Admin — title: "Admin" (e.g., data-testid: page-admin)

4) Route definitions
  - In src/App.tsx (or a dedicated src/routes/AppRoutes.tsx), wrap the application with <BrowserRouter> and define <Routes> as follows:
    - Parent route element={<MinimalLayout />}
      - <Route path="/login" element={<LoginPage />} />
      - <Route path="/profile" element={<ProfilePage />} />
      - <Route path="/dashboard" element={<DashboardPage />} />
      - <Route path="/new-brief" element={<NewBriefPage />} />
      - <Route path="/vault" element={<BrandVaultPage />} />
      - <Route path="/screening" element={<ScreeningRoomPage />} />
      - <Route path="/admin" element={<AdminPage />} />
  - Do not add redirect or guard logic. Do not modify 404 handling beyond existing behavior.

5) Type-safety and linting
  - Ensure all components are in TypeScript with FC typings or inferred JSX.Element return types.
  - Run lint and typecheck fixes as needed (npm run lint, npm run typecheck).

6) Manual verification (covers acceptance criteria)
  - Start the app and directly navigate to each route in the browser:
    - /login → title 'Login' is visible; a unique data-testid is present.
    - /profile → title 'Profile' is visible; a unique data-testid is present.
    - /dashboard → title 'Dashboard' is visible; a unique data-testid is present.
    - /new-brief → title 'New Brief' is visible; a unique data-testid is present.
    - /vault → title 'Brand Vault' is visible; a unique data-testid is present.
    - /screening → title 'Screening Room' is visible; a unique data-testid is present.
    - /admin → title 'Admin' is visible; a unique data-testid is present.
  - Confirm there are no redirects, no auth prompts, and each page renders within the shared minimal layout.

7) Delivery checklist
  - Commit new/updated files (e.g., MinimalLayout, 7 page components, App routes file) with concise messages.
  - Provide a short README snippet or PR description outlining:
    - The seven routes and page titles
    - Confirmation of unique data-testids per page
    - Explicit note that no global nav, guards, or business logic were added

messages.content: Hi! I’m ready to support the “Scaffold routes and independent placeholder pages for PoC screens” story. I have the product backlog and architecture context on hand. Are there any specific architecture entities or diagrams you’d like to highlight as most relevant to this work item?
I've provided the architectural context, please give me your understanding of this feature
Here’s my understanding of the Rivvy Portal UI scaffolding; I have 7 questions to finalize scope before spec.

Answers to your questions:

Q: What frontend stack and router are we targeting (e.g., React Router, Next.js App/Pages Router, Vue Router)?
A: React Router, by the way, this is a React/TS app

Q: Should titles be rendered as an H1 element and match the exact strings provided?
A: Sure but too detailed a question about html tags

Q: Where should the data-testid live (root page container vs. title), and is there a required naming convention?
A: No convention, don't add this to spec, the implementer will decide

Q: Is a shared minimal layout acceptable, or must each page render without any shared layout shell?
A: shared

Q: Confirm that all routes should be publicly accessible with no guards or redirects for the PoC.
A: confirmed

Q: Should we add missing UI screens (Profile, Dashboard, Screening Room, Admin) to the architecture model for parity?
A: No, they are they, don't worry about this

Q: Are there repository path or naming conventions we must follow for route and component files?
A: No
Thanks—your answers resolve my questions. I’m ready to proceed to spec with the scope and criteria below.
Okay, let's see if we need to break this work item into parts...
This item is small enough, so let's get the software architect to review this.
